content.text:
## Audit: production holders of the storage handle

The handle exists in two forms, and both are retained: `*storage.Storage` (the wrapper) and the raw `*sql.DB` it wraps. **20 retainers** in production code, none in tests. Grouped by area.

---

### 0. The root — `internal/storage`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `storage.Storage` | `internal/storage/storage.go:14` — `db *sql.DB` | The wrapper itself; built by `NewStorage(db *sql.DB)` at `internal/storage/storage.go:18`. Every other retainer below hangs off this one instance. |

**Four in-package builders retain the handle independently** — three of them copy the *raw* `*sql.DB` out of `Storage`, so they survive even if `Storage` is replaced. These are the sharpest stale-handle risk in the change:

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | `storage.batchBuilder` | `internal/storage/batch.go:18` — `db *sql.DB` | Builds feed-refresh job batches; **copies `s.db`** at `internal/storage/batch.go:27`, constructed via `Storage.NewBatchBuilder()` (`internal/storage/batch.go:25`). |
| 3 | `storage.feedQueryBuilder` | `internal/storage/feed_query_builder.go:19` — `db *sql.DB` | Builds feed SELECT queries; **copies the raw db**, constructed via `Storage.NewFeedQueryBuilder()` (`internal/storage/feed_query_builder.go:32`). |
| 4 | `storage.entryPaginationBuilder` | `internal/storage/entry_pagination_builder.go:19` — `db *sql.DB` | Builds entry prev/next pagination queries; **copies the raw db**, constructed at `internal/storage/entry_pagination_builder.go:205`. |
| 5 | `storage.EntryQueryBuilder` | `internal/storage/entry_query_builder.go:21` — `store *Storage` | Builds entry SELECT queries; retains the `*Storage` (not raw db), and is handed out to API/UI/fever/googlereader callers via `NewEntryQueryBuilder`. |
| 6 | `storage.certificateCache` | `internal/storage/certificate_cache.go:19` — `storage *Storage` | SQL-backed `autocert.Cache` for Let's Encrypt certs. Long-lived: installed into the autocert manager at `internal/http/server/server.go:147` and kept for the whole server lifetime. |

---

### 1. Web UI — `internal/ui`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 7 | `ui.handler` | `internal/ui/handler.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | The single UI request handler struct; all ~83 UI page methods (`showCategoryEntriesPage`, `saveAPIKey`, `refreshCategory`, …) go through `h.store`. |
| 8 | `ui.webSessionMiddleware` | `internal/ui/web_session_middleware.go:20` — `store *storage.Storage` | Loads/validates the web session from the cookie on every request; built at `internal/ui/web_session_middleware.go:24`. |
| 9 | `ui.authProxyMiddleware` | `internal/ui/auth_proxy_middleware.go:19` — `store *storage.Storage` | Auth-proxy header authentication (auto-creates/looks up the user); built at `internal/ui/auth_proxy_middleware.go:23`. |

---

### 2. REST API — `internal/api`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10 | `api.handler` | `internal/api/api.go:14` — `store *storage.Storage` | All `/v1/*` JSON endpoints; built at `internal/api/api.go:21`. |
| 11 | `api.middleware` | `internal/api/middleware.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | API-key and HTTP-Basic authentication (`validateAPIKeyAuth` `internal/api/middleware.go:37`, `validateBasicAuth` `internal/api/middleware.go:90`); built at `internal/api/middleware.go:20`. |

---

### 3. Google Reader API — `internal/googlereader`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 12 | `googlereader.greaderHandler` | `internal/googlereader/handler.go:69` — `store *storage.Storage` | All GReader endpoints (stream, edit-tag, subscribe, mark-all-read); built at `internal/googlereader/handler.go:40`. |
| 13 | `googlereader.authMiddleware` | `internal/googlereader/middleware.go:22` — `store *storage.Storage` | GReader auth-token validation; built at `internal/googlereader/middleware.go:27`. |

---

### 4. Fever API — `internal/fever`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 14 | `fever.feverHandler` | `internal/fever/handler.go:28` — `store *storage.Storage` | All Fever endpoints (`handleItems`, `handleUnreadItems`, `handleWriteItems`); built at `internal/fever/handler.go:23`. |

---

### 5. Background workers — `internal/worker`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 15 | `worker.worker` | `internal/worker/worker.go:20` — `store *storage.Storage` | One per background goroutine, refreshing feeds for the process lifetime (`w.store` used in `Run` at `internal/worker/worker.go:40`); N of them created at `internal/worker/pool.go:40`. Note `worker.Pool` (`internal/worker/pool.go:14`) does **not** retain it — only the workers do. |

---

### 6. Reader subsystems — `internal/reader`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 16 | `icon.iconChecker` | `internal/reader/icon/checker.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | Per-feed favicon fetch/store checker; built at `internal/reader/icon/checker.go:22`, short-lived but retains across `UpdateOrCreateFeedIcon`/`CreateFeedIconIfMissing`. |
| 17 | `opml.Handler` | `internal/reader/opml/handler.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | OPML import/export; built at `internal/reader/opml/handler.go:173`. |

---

### 7. Metrics — `internal/metric`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 18 | `metric.collector` | `internal/metric/metric.go:147` — `store *storage.Storage` | Prometheus collector; built at `internal/metric/metric.go:152` and then loops forever in `GatherStorageMetrics` (`internal/metric/metric.go:172`) polling `DBStats`. Longest-lived holder after the workers. |

---

### 8. Closure-captured retainers (no struct field — the static graph does not report these)

These keep the handle alive in a closure for the process lifetime, which is functionally identical to a field for your purposes:

| # | Holder | Capture site | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 19 | `server.newReadinessProbe` | `internal/http/server/healthcheck.go:18` → captured in the returned `http.HandlerFunc` at `internal/http/server/healthcheck.go:19` (`store.Ping()` at :20) | `/healthcheck` readiness probe; the closure is registered on the mux and outlives the call. |
| 20 | `fever.Middleware` | `internal/fever/middleware.go:17` → captured by the returned middleware closure | Fever API-key auth middleware, installed once into the router (`internal/http/server/routes.go:18`). |
| 21 | `cli.feedScheduler` / `cli.cleanupScheduler` | launched as goroutines at `internal/cli/scheduler.go:19` and `internal/cli/scheduler.go:28`; hold `store` in their frame across an infinite `time.Tick` loop (`internal/cli/scheduler.go:33`, `internal/cli/scheduler.go:53`) | Periodic feed-refresh batching and periodic cleanup tasks. They never return, so they pin whatever `*Storage` they were started with. |

---

### What this means for the contract change

- **Field-based holders (1–18)** are fixable by swapping the field type / re-injecting.
- **Items 2, 3, 4 are the trap**: they hold `*sql.DB` copied out of `Storage`, not `*Storage`. If your change makes `Storage` re-acquire or swap its `db`, those three builders keep the pre-swap handle. Either give them a `*Storage` back-reference (as `EntryQueryBuilder` already does at `internal/storage/entry_query_builder.go:21`) or make them fetch `db` per query.
- **Items 19–21 cannot be re-injected after startup** — they're captured, unaddressable closures/goroutines. If the new contract allows the handle to be replaced at runtime, these need an indirection (accessor func or a stable `*Storage` box) rather than a captured value.
- **Item 15** (`worker.worker`) and **item 18** (`metric.collector`) run for the whole process; a swapped handle leaves them stale silently — no request will surface the error.

Source: Sense composition graph on `storage.Storage` (14 `composed_by` entries), cross-checked with a literal grep for `*storage.Storage` and `*sql.DB` fields, which found the four in-package `*sql.DB` retainers the composition edge on `Storage` does not cover.

result:
## Audit: production holders of the storage handle

The handle exists in two forms, and both are retained: `*storage.Storage` (the wrapper) and the raw `*sql.DB` it wraps. **20 retainers** in production code, none in tests. Grouped by area.

---

### 0. The root — `internal/storage`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `storage.Storage` | `internal/storage/storage.go:14` — `db *sql.DB` | The wrapper itself; built by `NewStorage(db *sql.DB)` at `internal/storage/storage.go:18`. Every other retainer below hangs off this one instance. |

**Four in-package builders retain the handle independently** — three of them copy the *raw* `*sql.DB` out of `Storage`, so they survive even if `Storage` is replaced. These are the sharpest stale-handle risk in the change:

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | `storage.batchBuilder` | `internal/storage/batch.go:18` — `db *sql.DB` | Builds feed-refresh job batches; **copies `s.db`** at `internal/storage/batch.go:27`, constructed via `Storage.NewBatchBuilder()` (`internal/storage/batch.go:25`). |
| 3 | `storage.feedQueryBuilder` | `internal/storage/feed_query_builder.go:19` — `db *sql.DB` | Builds feed SELECT queries; **copies the raw db**, constructed via `Storage.NewFeedQueryBuilder()` (`internal/storage/feed_query_builder.go:32`). |
| 4 | `storage.entryPaginationBuilder` | `internal/storage/entry_pagination_builder.go:19` — `db *sql.DB` | Builds entry prev/next pagination queries; **copies the raw db**, constructed at `internal/storage/entry_pagination_builder.go:205`. |
| 5 | `storage.EntryQueryBuilder` | `internal/storage/entry_query_builder.go:21` — `store *Storage` | Builds entry SELECT queries; retains the `*Storage` (not raw db), and is handed out to API/UI/fever/googlereader callers via `NewEntryQueryBuilder`. |
| 6 | `storage.certificateCache` | `internal/storage/certificate_cache.go:19` — `storage *Storage` | SQL-backed `autocert.Cache` for Let's Encrypt certs. Long-lived: installed into the autocert manager at `internal/http/server/server.go:147` and kept for the whole server lifetime. |

---

### 1. Web UI — `internal/ui`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 7 | `ui.handler` | `internal/ui/handler.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | The single UI request handler struct; all ~83 UI page methods (`showCategoryEntriesPage`, `saveAPIKey`, `refreshCategory`, …) go through `h.store`. |
| 8 | `ui.webSessionMiddleware` | `internal/ui/web_session_middleware.go:20` — `store *storage.Storage` | Loads/validates the web session from the cookie on every request; built at `internal/ui/web_session_middleware.go:24`. |
| 9 | `ui.authProxyMiddleware` | `internal/ui/auth_proxy_middleware.go:19` — `store *storage.Storage` | Auth-proxy header authentication (auto-creates/looks up the user); built at `internal/ui/auth_proxy_middleware.go:23`. |

---

### 2. REST API — `internal/api`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10 | `api.handler` | `internal/api/api.go:14` — `store *storage.Storage` | All `/v1/*` JSON endpoints; built at `internal/api/api.go:21`. |
| 11 | `api.middleware` | `internal/api/middleware.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | API-key and HTTP-Basic authentication (`validateAPIKeyAuth` `internal/api/middleware.go:37`, `validateBasicAuth` `internal/api/middleware.go:90`); built at `internal/api/middleware.go:20`. |

---

### 3. Google Reader API — `internal/googlereader`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 12 | `googlereader.greaderHandler` | `internal/googlereader/handler.go:69` — `store *storage.Storage` | All GReader endpoints (stream, edit-tag, subscribe, mark-all-read); built at `internal/googlereader/handler.go:40`. |
| 13 | `googlereader.authMiddleware` | `internal/googlereader/middleware.go:22` — `store *storage.Storage` | GReader auth-token validation; built at `internal/googlereader/middleware.go:27`. |

---

### 4. Fever API — `internal/fever`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 14 | `fever.feverHandler` | `internal/fever/handler.go:28` — `store *storage.Storage` | All Fever endpoints (`handleItems`, `handleUnreadItems`, `handleWriteItems`); built at `internal/fever/handler.go:23`. |

---

### 5. Background workers — `internal/worker`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 15 | `worker.worker` | `internal/worker/worker.go:20` — `store *storage.Storage` | One per background goroutine, refreshing feeds for the process lifetime (`w.store` used in `Run` at `internal/worker/worker.go:40`); N of them created at `internal/worker/pool.go:40`. Note `worker.Pool` (`internal/worker/pool.go:14`) does **not** retain it — only the workers do. |

---

### 6. Reader subsystems — `internal/reader`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 16 | `icon.iconChecker` | `internal/reader/icon/checker.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | Per-feed favicon fetch/store checker; built at `internal/reader/icon/checker.go:22`, short-lived but retains across `UpdateOrCreateFeedIcon`/`CreateFeedIconIfMissing`. |
| 17 | `opml.Handler` | `internal/reader/opml/handler.go:17` — `store *storage.Storage` | OPML import/export; built at `internal/reader/opml/handler.go:173`. |

---

### 7. Metrics — `internal/metric`

| # | Holder | Retaining field | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 18 | `metric.collector` | `internal/metric/metric.go:147` — `store *storage.Storage` | Prometheus collector; built at `internal/metric/metric.go:152` and then loops forever in `GatherStorageMetrics` (`internal/metric/metric.go:172`) polling `DBStats`. Longest-lived holder after the workers. |

---

### 8. Closure-captured retainers (no struct field — the static graph does not report these)

These keep the handle alive in a closure for the process lifetime, which is functionally identical to a field for your purposes:

| # | Holder | Capture site | What it is |
|---|---|---|---|
| 19 | `server.newReadinessProbe` | `internal/http/server/healthcheck.go:18` → captured in the returned `http.HandlerFunc` at `internal/http/server/healthcheck.go:19` (`store.Ping()` at :20) | `/healthcheck` readiness probe; the closure is registered on the mux and outlives the call. |
| 20 | `fever.Middleware` | `internal/fever/middleware.go:17` → captured by the returned middleware closure | Fever API-key auth middleware, installed once into the router (`internal/http/server/routes.go:18`). |
| 21 | `cli.feedScheduler` / `cli.cleanupScheduler` | launched as goroutines at `internal/cli/scheduler.go:19` and `internal/cli/scheduler.go:28`; hold `store` in their frame across an infinite `time.Tick` loop (`internal/cli/scheduler.go:33`, `internal/cli/scheduler.go:53`) | Periodic feed-refresh batching and periodic cleanup tasks. They never return, so they pin whatever `*Storage` they were started with. |

---

### What this means for the contract change

- **Field-based holders (1–18)** are fixable by swapping the field type / re-injecting.
- **Items 2, 3, 4 are the trap**: they hold `*sql.DB` copied out of `Storage`, not `*Storage`. If your change makes `Storage` re-acquire or swap its `db`, those three builders keep the pre-swap handle. Either give them a `*Storage` back-reference (as `EntryQueryBuilder` already does at `internal/storage/entry_query_builder.go:21`) or make them fetch `db` per query.
- **Items 19–21 cannot be re-injected after startup** — they're captured, unaddressable closures/goroutines. If the new contract allows the handle to be replaced at runtime, these need an indirection (accessor func or a stable `*Storage` box) rather than a captured value.
- **Item 15** (`worker.worker`) and **item 18** (`metric.collector`) run for the whole process; a swapped handle leaves them stale silently — no request will surface the error.
